Config/Action: Set OUTBOUND_EMAIL_ENABLED=false and EMAIL_PROVIDER=disabled; restart app/worker if needed.
Expected Queue Effect: Pending Email rows remain pending/paused; retry_count unchanged; no dead-letter.
Risk if Fails: Pending customer replies could be marked failed/dead.

Expected Queue Effect: All external provider dispatch stops; no new claims.
Expected Business Effect: Emergency stop for all external outbound channels.
Risk if Fails: External sends may continue during incident.

Config/Action: Admin deactivate Email account or set health offline.
Expected Queue Effect: New Email capability becomes not ready; existing queued rows use no disabled account.
Expected Business Effect: Stops bad sender while preserving queued data.
Risk if Fails: Agents may continue using compromised account.

Config/Action: Disable EMAIL_DELIVERY_EVENTS_ENABLED and rotate webhook secret.
Expected Queue Effect: Webhook writes stop; no provider events accepted.

Config/Action: Disable EMAIL_INBOUND_ENABLED; leave raw storage enabled if safe.
Expected Queue Effect: Inbound messages stop linking; unresolved backlog preserved.
Expected Business Effect: Prevents wrong ticket association during incident.
Risk if Fails: Customer messages may link to wrong tickets.

Config/Action: Hide Email channel in capability/UI by disabling OUTBOUND_EMAIL_ENABLED.
Expected Queue Effect: Agents no longer see Email as sendable; backend remains fail-closed.
Expected Business Effect: Stops operator exposure while backend remains intact.
Verification Command: Open ticket reply panel and capability endpoint
Risk if Fails: Agents may send through broken UI.
